description: MCP-38 threat taxonomy (arxiv 2025): tool-poisoning, parasitic tool-chaining, server-identity spoofing, capability lying, prompt injection via tool description. Applies to Claude Desktop, Cursor, VS Code Copilot Chat, any MCP-aware host. Use against MCP server HTTP/SSE/stdio entry-points OR against an LLM agent that consumes MCP tool listings.

tool_description_prompt_injection.description: MCP servers expose tool descriptions during the listTools handshake. A malicious server smuggles instructions in the description; the client's LLM incorporates them as system-prompt extensions and changes behavior.

probes.description: Fingerprint MCP server. Inspect tool descriptions for instruction-like content.

parasitic_tool_chaining.description: Malicious server registers a tool whose description claims 'use this tool before any other'. Client LLM calls it on every request; server harvests context. Detection: large fan-out in tool-call telemetry for a single tool with no business purpose.

probes.description: Pattern-match tool descriptions for 'always call', 'before any', 'must use'

server_identity_spoofing.description: Two MCP servers register identically-named tools; client LLM dispatches based on user prompt with no host disambiguation. Attacker server siphons calls intended for the legitimate server.

probes.description: Audit listTools output for collisions in registered tool names across servers

rug_pull_post_install.description: MCP server changes tool descriptions or behavior after the user has approved it (Cursor/Claude Desktop only validate descriptions at install time, not on each call).

probes.description: Detect by comparing tool description hash across sessions

indirect_resource_injection.description: MCP server returns a resource that contains an instruction the host LLM acts on (analogous to EchoLeak but channelled through MCP resources/read).

probes.description: Read a likely-attacker-controlled resource, scan for embedded directives
